instrument_provenance.smoke_verification_note: lm_attractor_probe_rd.py --smoke items [1]-[5] (the entire chunk_key_gram_stats/summarize_gram_records positive/negative/EOT-exclusion/min-valid/multi-head battery) verified PASSING under this exact stub, this session, before this sim was written. Item [6] (a real DeltaNetLM forward pass) was not exercised and is not needed here.

anchors_check_against_archived_numbers.note: SCALE_TRANSFER_DESIGN.md sec 5.9/5.10 quotes 7.94/63.50 at K=64,d=64 -- matches to the printed decimal places.

calibrated_baseline_vs_archived_referenc.note: The calibrated regime's own baseline (no bias applied) is built to resemble the REAL archived Arm-1 span_frac (0.248 at 14M up to 0.389 at 392M), not the degenerate near-zero value the naive i.i.d.-random regime gives by construction.

regime_naive_iid_random_baseline.description: shared_frac=0.0 -- baseline span_frac ~0 (degenerate, close to the anchors()['random'] reference point itself, NOT representative of a real trained checkpoint's Arm-1 baseline).

direction_summary.direction_verdict: ABOVE (span_frac RISES under dense per-token blending)

regime_calibrated_to_archived_baseline.description: shared_frac=0.6 -- baseline span_frac calibrated to ~0.270, matching the REAL archived Arm-1 reference range (0.248-0.389). THIS IS THE PRIMARY, DECISION-RELEVANT REGIME.

direction_summary.direction_verdict: BELOW (span_frac FALLS under dense per-token blending)

crossover_scan.description: Sweeps the baseline's own non-orthonormality (shared_frac, x-axis) and reports where the sign of (Arm2 dense-blended span_frac - baseline span_frac) flips. Resolves the apparent naive-vs-calibrated contradiction: both are the SAME code, different points on one continuous axis.
